Ahead, the clarification of this subject occurred like this: 'And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear. And you see them looking at you while they do not see' (Ayah 198). 'It is all the same for you whether you invite them or you are silent.' Meaning wail, cry out, plead, or remain silent—both are equal for them.

Indeed, those you call upon besides Allah are servants like yourselves. So call upon them and let them respond to you, if you are truthful. Meaning they themselves are God's creatures just like you; so what help or guidance can they give you? And if your claim is this—that they can help and guide you—then call them, let them help you. This is a challenge to the polytheists just like the type of challenge in 'And call your witnesses besides Allah, if you are truthful' (Baqarah: 23). The meaning became: until now, you accepted whatever you wished regarding your these imaginary deities and had others accept it, but now the Prophet and the Quran have challenged the divinity of all of them. Now is the time that they reach for your help and guidance and handle you and themselves too. If they saved their divinity, then certainly it will be known that you are truthful. It should be clear that the idols the Arabs used to worship—according to their assumption—were idols of angels, jinn, and stars; for this reason He called them 'servants like yourselves' ('ibadun amthalukum). See the detail of this in our book 'Haqiqat-e-Shirk' (The Reality of Polytheism).

Indeed, my protector is Allah, who has sent down the Book; and He is the protector of the righteous. We have clarified the meaning of 'Wali' (protector/guardian) at another place—it comes in the meaning of supporter and helper and resort and taskmaster. Above in Ayahs 192–193 it has passed—that these idols whom these polytheists worship—what help will they give them, they cannot even help themselves; and if these people call them for help and victory and support and guidance, then their calling and not calling are both equal; they will never reach for their help and guidance. Now, from the tongue of the Prophet (PBUH), the glory of Allah (Glorified be He) is being described—that my supporter and helper is that Allah who has sent down the Book with great special-arrangement for my guidance and the guidance of the creation, and who loves His virtuous and righteous servants and helps and guides them at every stage. At another place we have clarified that in 'nazzala' (sent down) the subject of special-arrangement is found. Meaning He has sent down the Book with extreme care for the guidance of His servants. Although a servant is in need of his Lord for everything, but his greatest need is for guidance, the arrangement of which God alone has made; so finally for what ailment are these idols and gods a remedy and for what need is their worship taking place?
And those you call upon besides Him are unable to help you, nor can they help themselves. This subject of Ayah 192 above has been described with a slight change of style.

And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear; and you see them looking at you while they do not see. This is clarification of the subject of Ayah 193 above; here the word 'la-yasma'u' (they do not hear) is shedding light on the correct meaning of 'la-yattabi'ukum' (they do not follow you). 

An example of eloquence of manifestation: 'And you see them looking at you while they do not see.' In this portion, the words 'ru'yah' (vision), 'nazar' (look), and 'ibsar' (sight) have been used with such excellence that it is simply the inimitability (i'jaz) of the Quran. The meaning is: O sightless ones, you are imagining that these are looking at you with a fixed gaze, whereas they perceive and see nothing at all. 

In 'tara-hum' (you see them), the address to the singular is contextual in the meaning of plural, and the addressees are the polytheists.

Take [what is given of] forgiveness, and enjoin what is right, and turn away from the ignorant. 

Some instructions to the Prophet and the Muslims: Now at the end of the Surah, some timely suitable instructions have been given to the Prophet (PBUH) and through you to the Muslims. The word 'Afw' (forgiveness/surplus), as we clarified at another place, when it comes in such a context, does not mean 'forgiving from the heart' but means merely 'overlooking'. 'Urf' (recognized good) is that matter which is recognized/well-known according to intellect and nature and reasonable people. Here, by it is meant those same points of Monotheism and Resurrection and virtue and justice toward which the Arabs were being invited in that era and which were entirely based on the testimony of intellect and nature and were the 'voice of the heart' for those of sound nature. He stated: you remain firm upon the call you are giving of Monotheism and Resurrection, virtue and justice, and for now overlook the nonsensical talk (zhaz-khai) of these ignorant ones. Soon that time is coming when they will see the result of these futilities of theirs themselves.

And if an evil whisper comes to you from Satan, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is Hearing and Knowing. 'Nazgh' (evil whisper/impulse) means to cause a sting, to cause a prick, to inflict a wound. This strategy has been described—for remaining firm on the path of overlooking and for turning away from the way of the ignorant and for keeping one's heart safe from the shocks of their pricks and taunts. The meaning is: if any shock reaches the heart from the 'sham-speech' and 'dust-play' of these devils from among jinn and humans, then seek the refuge of your Lord; your Lord is All-Hearing, All-Knowing; whenever you return toward Him, He hears your prayers and pleas, He knows your circumstances and worries, and He is also well-aware of the fitnah-instigations and aggressions of the devils; He will remove your every grief.

Indeed, those who fear [Allah]—when an impulse from Satan touches them, they remember [Allah] and at once they have insight. 

'Indeed, those who fear [Allah]'. In 'Khudh al-'afwa', although the address is to the singular, but this is, as we hinted, through the Holy Prophet (PBUH) to all the Muslims; thus in this verse the style of plural clarified this hidden reality. He stated: those who adopt the path of piety (taqwa) instead of ignorance—whenever some shock from the ignorance of the ignorant and the devilry of the devils hits them—they remember their Lord, by which immediately their internal-self becomes enlightened and despite all the 'dust-play' of the wicked and the enemies, their path does not go out of sight from their eyes. This, as it were, has told the method and benefit of that 'Istia'adhah' (seeking refuge) which was instructed in the verse above—that the method of entering God's refuge is for a person to remember his Lord; this thing will create such vision and such power and courage within the heart that suddenly all the dust will clear from in front of the eyes.

But their brothers extend them in error; then they do not stop. The referent of 'hum' (their/them) are those 'jahilin' (ignorant ones) whose mention passed above in Ayah 199; by 'brothers' (ikhwan), their those companions are meant in whose hands their reins are—regardless of whether they be from the devils of humans or the devils of jinn. He stated: as for the believers, the remembrance of God handles them, but for the ignorant ones, their devils—while making them wander in the valleys of misguidance—bring them to the final destination; they do not leave even a minor deficiency that any possibility of return remains.

And when you do not bring them a sign, they say, 'Why have you not devised it?' Say, 'I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord. This [Quran] is enlightenment from your Lord and guidance and mercy for a people who believe.' 

Satanic taunts and their confrontation: This is an example of those satanic taunts and impulses (nazghat) with which the Holy Prophet (PBUH) had to deal every time at the hands of the disbelievers. The meaning is: when you do not show a miracle upon their demand according to their selection, they taunt you: 'Why have you not picked it up from somewhere?' By this statement, the intention of the wrongdoers would be: as far as the Quran is concerned, its matter is very easy; those points comprised of the events of the predecessors and the traditions of the soothsayers and the People of the Book which fell into the ears from here and there—among them those points which pleased the heart were joined together and some discourse was made and it was brought and recited to us with this claim that 'Allah has sent revelation through His special angel'; but now that we have put the demand of the miracle, you are not successful because picking and choosing from here and there is one thing, showing a miracle is another thing. This was not a thing of picking/choosing that you would have picked and chosen from somewhere and shown us that 'here, your demand is fulfilled'. As it were, in this taunt, there is not only the taunt of not being able to show the miracle, but even a more poisonous taunt is latent within it: that, God forbid, the Quran is a forged thing which is a collection of things picked/chosen from here and there according to one's taste. 

The actual literal meaning of 'Ijtaba'' is to select and pick out some thing from a collection, but when this word is used in the context of sarcasm, as is here, its meaning will become 'to forge' and 'to make/invent'; for this reason Farra has explained it as 'aftalaytaha' (you invented it) and 'aftaraytaha' (you forged it), and this Tafseer is correct according to us. In the sarcastic style, the meanings of words change. 

'Say, I only follow what is revealed to me'. This is the Prophet's response to the mentioned sarcasm of the disbelievers, and see what a dignified and full response it is. He said: Tell them you believe what you wish and say what you wish, I am only following this revelation which comes upon me from the side of my Lord. The instruction of 'turning away from the ignorance of the ignorant' which was given above—this answer is the best example of the compliance with that instruction. No notice at all was taken of their ignorant talk; only the actual reality was manifest in an extremely simple but extremely majestic style. 'This [Quran] is enlightenment from your Lord'—meaning you call this thing a thing forged by me, but these are the verses that lift the veils of the eyes and the hearts from the side of your Lord and are guidance and mercy for you. Guidance with respect to its beginning and mercy with respect to its end. But these are beneficial for those who believe. In 'for a people who believe' (li-qawmin yu'minun), this subtle allusion is also latent: that there is no defect in what I am presenting; the actual defect is within your own hearts—that you do not want to accept the reality.

So when the Quran is recited, then listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy. 

The method of benefiting from the Quran: Now He told what the method of benefiting from the guidance and enlightenment of the Quran is; He stated: when the Quran is recited, then listen to it with attention and for it, pay attention with silence. If you do this, Divine mercy will turn toward you and your hearts will open to believe in it. 'Insat' means to pay attention with silence to hear someone's talk. In it, there is a hint toward that wrong behavior of the disbelievers which they had adopted in the matter of the Quran. In Surah Fussilat it is: 'And those who disbelieve say, Do not listen to this Quran and make noise therein' (Fussilat: 26). He stated: this method is the method of ignorance, the result of which is deprivation of mercy. The correct method is exactly opposite to it, and it is to listen to it and, instead of noise and clamor, listen by paying attention with silence. This is the method for benefiting from this mercy. 

A group of our jurists has made this verse a source for an argument for not reciting Fatihah behind the Imam, but this verse, as is clear, is not a verse of this context; and even if setting aside the context, someone wishes to make some such deduction from the verse, the point does not go in favor of those people who are absolute opponents of reciting Fatihah behind the Imam, but goes in favor of those people who stop from reciting Fatihah in audible (jahri) prayers but do not stop in silent (sirri) ones.

And remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear and without loudness in words in the mornings and the evenings. And do not be among the heedless. 

The method of remembering God: Above, remembering God was told as the method of coming into God's refuge. Now, the method of remembering God is being told. For it, three points were stated. One is—that it should be in heart of hearts with humility, poverty, entreaty, and fear. From this it was known that pride, humility, and the feeling of deservingness and arrogance is contrary to it. Second is—that if there is remembrance with words, it should be 'duna al-jahr' (without loudness), i.e., it should not be with very much loud voice. The clarification of this point in Surah Bani Israel occurred like this: 'And do not recite [too] loudly in your prayer or [too] quietly but seek between that a way' (Bani Israel: 110). This instruction was given both to save from the method of the polytheists and for the reason that our Sustainer is All-Hearing, All-Knowing—God forbid, He is not deaf. It would be remembered that on one occasion, the Prophet (PBUH) had also stopped the Companions (RA) from remembering with too loud a voice. Besides, in it there is also a greater possibility of ostentation, fame, and display, which are things entirely contrary to sincerity (ikhlas). Third is—that the remembrance of Allah should be at all times. The words 'in the mornings and the evenings' (bi-al-ghuduwi wa-al-asal), as we clarified at another place, are proof of the subject of 'encompassing'. Just as we speak the words 'morning and evening' (subh-o-sham), these are those words in Arabic. 

'And do not be among the heedless' is for emphasis of this same meaning. Meaning, there should be no heedlessness from the remembrance of God at any time. Just as for the life of the body, the coming and going of breath is necessary, similarly for the life of the spirit, Divine remembrance is necessary. Satan remains at all times in the wait for attack, he is not heedless from this work at any time; for this reason, from that sanctuary which is for the attainment of refuge from him, the person should also not be heedless at any time. The forms and shapes of this remembrance change due to the change of circumstances, necessities, requirements, and times, but heedlessness from it is not permissible at any time. Wherever the person became heedless, Satan will attack from some way or the other. There is no need for a reminder here that although the address is in the singular form, but its nature is the same as described above under Ayah 199.

Indeed, those who are near your Lord are not too proud to worship Him, and they exalt Him, and to Him they prostrate. 

From 'those who are near your Lord', the hint is toward the angels. Regarding them, He stated that they never show defiance from God's servitude at any time, they remain constantly engaged in His exaltation (tasbih), and they prostrate to Him. This verse, on one hand, tells of the rank of those who remain constantly active in Allah's remembrance—that those people who remember God at all times, although they live and dwell in the earth, but their connection becomes with the 'Holy Gathering' of the angels. Because just as they remain constantly humming/chanting exaltation (tasbih) and glorification (tahlil), these too remain similarly engaged in Divine remembrance. On the other hand, in it is also an irony/sarcasm upon the polytheists—that these strut about merely on the basis of the intercession of the angels, neither bringing God into care nor the Prophet; but the state of the angels themselves is this: that they are engaged in exaltation and prostration before God at all times. 

These are the final lines which were recorded in the Tafseer of Surah A'raf. The points which emerged correctly from the pen, they emerged with the tawfiq (success/ability) of Allah the Almighty. Those which emerged wrongly are the result of my lack of knowledge. May Allah the Almighty keep me and the readers of this book safe from their harm. And our final prayer is that praise be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.
